overview: Archmantic reverse-engineers a single grounded architecture model (the IR) from a repo and serves it to both humans — interactive diagrams plus an AI-curated map — and AI agents, over MCP. A tiered, cheapest-first pipeline collects everything with file:line provenance; projections render it as context / component / sequence / ERD views and a domain map; the spec layer (project brain, features) and skills add human intent on top of the structure. The same model an agent queries is the one a human edits.

analyze.name: Analysis pipeline
analyze.description: Tiered, cheapest-first reverse-engineering (Tier 0/1 + stack, data model, endpoints, groups) that turns a repo into the grounded model.

ir.name: Architecture model (IR)
ir.description: The core typed model every element is grounded into; all diagrams and MCP answers are projections of it.

mcp.name: MCP server
mcp.description: Exposes the model to AI agents as read/write tools over stdio, with per-call usage metering.

skills.name: Skills
skills.description: On-shelf playbook catalog resolved against the model with cited reasons.

diff.name: Diff & history
diff.description: Architecture diff, per-commit history, and PR-comment rendering.

cloud.name: Team cloud
cloud.description: Push/pull the shared model and usage events to Neon — direct, or via the org-scoped API.

project.name: Spec & projections
project.description: The human-intent layer (project brain, features, curation) and the projections: HTML viewer, BPMN, build spec, AGENTS.md.

root.name: CLI & orchestration
root.description: The CLI entrypoint and top-level flows: analyze, agent hand-off, the local editor, and the multi-repo system view.
